title: Aloo Fry

subs.name: omit entirely
subs.notes: still excellent — a no-onion-no-garlic version is popular during fasts

subs.name: a squeeze of lemon juice at the end
subs.notes: add off-heat so the lemon doesn't turn bitter

steps.text: Slice potatoes about 3 mm thin — uniform thickness ensures even cooking. Pat dry with a cloth if they are wet from washing, or they will steam instead of fry.
Heat oil in a wide kadai on medium-high. Add cumin seeds and let them sizzle for 10 seconds. Add green chilli and onion (if using) and fry 2 minutes.

Add potato slices in a single layer (or as close to it as possible). Add turmeric, chilli powder, coriander powder, and salt. Toss to coat and spread flat. Do not stir for 3 minutes so the bottom layer develops a crust.

Flip the potatoes in sections — not individual slices — and press down gently. Cook another 5–7 minutes on medium, turning every 2 minutes, until most slices are golden and cooked through when pierced with a knife.
Sprinkle amchur over if using and toss once. Taste for salt. Garnish with coriander leaves and serve immediately alongside dal and rice or stuffed into roti.

cross_contact_notes: Ingredient metadata cannot prove an allergen-free kitchen. Check packaged labels and cross-contact statements for your products.
trust.provenance_summary: AI-assisted original draft; declared licence original.
trust.substitution_warning: A substitution can introduce allergens or change dietary suitability. Never claim a swap is safe without checking the replacement's label and the user's allergy context.

substitution_notes: amchur → a squeeze of lemon juice at the end (add off-heat so the lemon doesn't turn bitter); potato → raw banana (vazhakkai), sliced (same method, slightly earthier flavour); onion → omit entirely (still excellent — a no-onion-no-garlic version is popular during fasts)
